Title: Tripterygium wilfordii cytochrome P450s catalyze the methyl shift and epoxidations in the biosynthesis of triptonide

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances triptonide-pathway CYP activity and strongly increases triptonide-pathway intermediate/product accumulation, especially increasing triptonide output in yeast by more than 9-fold in the four-CYP pathway context.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among six T. wilfordii cytochrome b5 genes tested, only Twcytb5-A substantially stimulated the pathway. 2. Coexpression with TwCYP82D274/TwCYP71BE85/TwCYP71BE86/TwCYP82D213 increased levels of pathway intermediates and products in S. cerevisiae, with the strongest effect on TwCYP71BE86-associated products. 3. The effect was host-dependent: generally positive in yeast, but mixed or even negative for some intermediates in N. benthamiana.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae and transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with metabolite profiling by GC-MS/LC-qTOF-MS; comparison across six Twcytb5 isoforms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive triepoxide diterpenoid with pharmaceutical potential, including non-hormonal male contraceptive activity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene for pathway engineering and fermentation optimization of triptonide/triepoxide diterpenoid production in yeast; should be coexpressed with the core CYP cassette, but host-specific effects should be considered.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterologous host context (Saccharomyces cerevisiae versus Nicotiana benthamiana)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Host-dependent effects; in N. benthamiana coexpression reduced accumulation of some intermediates (8, 6, 7) in certain combinations, suggesting pathway imbalance or altered CYP electron partitioning.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous coexpression in yeast and tobacco leaves
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public T. wilfordii RNA-seq deposits used for candidate gene discovery; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an electron-transfer partner rather than a catalyst of the carbon skeleton modifications itself; five other T. wilfordii cytochrome b5 proteins did not show comparable stimulation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports downstream functionality of TwCYP enzymes in yeast, enabling oxidation steps leading to triptonide-pathway intermediates and final product formation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwPOR1 was identified from T. wilfordii transcriptomes and integrated into yeast to support TwCYP activity. 2. It functioned as the reductase partner in the heterologous S. cerevisiae system used to reconstruct the pathway. 3. Its presence was part of the functional platform for triptonide biosynthesis in yeast but it was not itself the main experimental subject for metabolite specificity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome integration into Saccharomyces cerevisiae together with TwCYPs and metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive triepoxide diterpenoid with pharmaceutical potential, including non-hormonal male contraceptive activity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required redox partner for reconstructing the TwCYP pathway in yeast production strains.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: T. wilfordii transcriptome BLAST search; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: General CYP electron donor used to support the heterologous pathway; not claimed as pathway-specific determinant of product chemistry.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early/mid oxygenation steps from miltiradiene toward abeo-abietane triepoxide diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive triepoxide diterpenoid with pharmaceutical and contraceptive potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates oxidation of miltiradiene-derived scaffold and is required, together with three additional TwCYPs, for triptonide formation; promotes accumulation of 14-hydroxy-dehydroabietadiene and downstream oxygenated intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates miltiradiene/dehydroabietadiene-derived substrate on the C-ring to form 14-hydroxy-dehydroabietadiene and additional oxygenated intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms early pathway intermediate required for downstream rearrangement and epoxidation toward triptonide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Of 68 candidate TwCYPs, TwCYP82D274 was the first identified enzyme that depleted miltiradiene and produced 14-hydroxy-dehydroabietadiene (5). 2. In N. benthamiana it also led to 3-epi-triptobenzene B and other oxygenated diterpenoids, while in yeast the main consensus product was 5. 3. It is one of the four CYPs constituting the minimal triptonide biosynthetic gene set.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Isolated from cDNA derived from root, stem and leaf tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves and engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: T. wilfordii tissues/root extracts (triptonide in native plant); heterologous accumulation in yeast and tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential entry CYP for reconstructing the triptonide pathway in heterologous hosts; should be stacked with TwCYP71BE86, TwCYP71BE85, TwCYP82D213 and support redox partners for fermentation production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In planta some products may reflect endogenous N. benthamiana activities; discussed as the first CYP82D family member shown to oxygenate diterpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Individual and combinatorial heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae with GC-MS/LC-qTOF-MS metabolite profiling; NMR identification of products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key upstream pathway enzyme, but explicit rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public T. wilfordii RNA-seq data used for candidate identification; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host system effects differ between N. benthamiana and S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product profile differs between hosts; broad product promiscuity may generate shunt products.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: A-ring rearrangement/lactone-forming branch toward abeo-abietane scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive triepoxide diterpenoid with pharmaceutical and contraceptive potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the unprecedented 18(4→3) methyl shift generating the abeo-abietane backbone and contributes to oxygenations leading to triptophenolide/lactone-containing intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes A-ring oxygenation-associated carbon rearrangement of the abietane scaffold, producing the 18(4→3) abeo-abietane core and downstream oxygenated intermediates such as abeo-abietane diol/aldehyde derivatives; contributes to formation of triptophenolide with TwCYP71BE85.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 18(4→3) abeo-abietane intermediates including compounds 6 and 7; contributes to triptophenolide (8) formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: creates the rearranged scaffold essential for downstream triptonide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwCYP71BE86 was shown in both N. benthamiana and S. cerevisiae to catalyze the unprecedented 18(4→3) abeo-abietane methyl shift. 2. Coexpression with TwCYP82D274 generated rearranged intermediates including 6, 7 and low amounts of 8 in planta. 3. In yeast, TwCYP71BE86 was necessary for formation of the abeo-abietane backbone, while TwCYP71BE85 was additionally required for efficient lactone formation to 8.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Isolated from T. wilfordii cDNA; close homologs found in tandem repeats in the genome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves and engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: T. wilfordii root tissue/native plant for related abeo-abietane diterpenoids; heterologous accumulation in yeast and tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical scaffold-diversifying enzyme for engineering abeo-abietane diterpenoids and triptonide production; high-value target for pathway reconstruction and mechanistic biocatalysis exploitation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents a highly unusual plant CYP reaction, likely involving Wagner-Meerwein-type rearrangement; broad product promiscuity and multiple catalytic cycles are suggested.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae, metabolite profiling, NMR structure elucidation of intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: branch-defining key control step for establishment of the abeo-abietane scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public T. wilfordii RNA-seq data used for candidate discovery; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host-dependent product distribution; activity strongly enhanced in yeast by Twcytb5-A
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate/product promiscuity yields multiple shunt products; incomplete oxygenation cycles may divert flux.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive triepoxide diterpenoid with pharmaceutical and contraceptive potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Works with TwCYP71BE86 in A-ring oxygenation and is required in yeast for lactone ring formation leading to triptophenolide, a key intermediate en route to triptonide.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes A-ring oxygenation steps contributing to lactone ring formation on the abeo-abietane scaffold, especially in cooperation with TwCYP71BE86.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor/intermediate for downstream epoxidation to triptonide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwCYP71BE85 individually oxygenated miltiradiene-derived diterpenes but generated fewer products than TwCYP71BE86. 2. Coexpression of TwCYP71BE85 with TwCYP71BE86 significantly increased triptophenolide (8) accumulation in planta. 3. In yeast, TwCYP71BE85 was required together with TwCYP71BE86 for lactone formation and production of 8.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves and engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Native T. wilfordii for downstream diterpenoids; heterologous accumulation in yeast and tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary component of the four-CYP cassette for efficient reconstruction of the triptonide pathway in microbial production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its most clearly resolved role is host-supported lactone formation in conjunction with TwCYP71BE86; substrate order remains not fully resolved.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae with LC-qTOF-MS/GC-MS and NMR-supported metabolite assignment
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key mid-pathway enzyme for lactone formation, but not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public T. wilfordii RNA-seq data used for candidate discovery; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host system affects observed product outcomes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuous oxygenation may contribute to side products.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late B/C-ring epoxidation branch completing triepoxide formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive triepoxide diterpenoid with pharmaceutical and contraceptive potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes the biosynthetic pathway to triptonide when coexpressed with TwCYP82D274, TwCYP71BE86 and TwCYP71BE85.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes late oxygenation/epoxidation steps on the B/C-ring system required for final triptonide formation, together with TwCYP82D274.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Late lactone-containing abeo-abietane intermediate(s), likely including triptophenolide (8) or closely related intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product in terminal epoxidation sequence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwCYP82D213 was selected from TwCYP82D homologs as a candidate for epoxide-forming oxygenation steps. 2. Coexpression with TwCYP82D274, TwCYP71BE86 and TwCYP71BE85 resulted in triptonide accumulation in both N. benthamiana and S. cerevisiae. 3. It therefore completes the minimal four-CYP set for heterologous triptonide biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves and engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: T. wilfordii native tissues; heterologous accumulation in yeast and tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal CYP for reconstitution of triptonide production platforms; should be combined with the three upstream TwCYPs and suitable redox partners.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed to participate in highly coordinated consecutive epoxidation cycles; pathway intermediates with one or two epoxides were not observed, suggesting unstable or channeled intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae with metabolite detection of triptonide by LC-qTOF-MS
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public T. wilfordii RNA-seq data used for candidate discovery; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host-independent confirmation in both tobacco and yeast
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Late epoxidation may require highly coordinated catalysis; inefficient turnover may contribute to quinone/shunt products.
